title: crocodilodes glabrata
intro: Crocodilodes glabrata, also known as the "sensitive plant," is a type of tropical legume native to Central and South America, characterized by its compound leaves that fold up when touched or disturbed. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Crocodilodes glabrata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Herbal preparations of Crocodilodes glabrata include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of skin and health issues.
